hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The valid contract categories are: invalid_scalar, incorrect_type, missing_property, unknown_property, invalid_property_value, unexpected_null, unknown_type, missing_type_tag, forbidden_anchor_or_alias, duplicate_key, empty_document, malformed_yaml, and max_alias.
In this implementation, '.inf' is mapped to JavaScript's Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY (or the specific infinity literal defined in your codebase's float parser).

context_pointers.actual_meaning: The implementation that converts '0xFF' or '0o77' into a raw integer without enforcing the 'long' shape constraint from the schema.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_1_encode_objects.json.description: Encode a structured object to a block mapping; nested objects are indented by the configured indentation size, and embedded lists and maps are nested under their key.

feature10_2_encode_defaults.json.description: Encoding default-valued properties is on by default; turning it off omits any property whose value equals its default, yielding an empty mapping when every property is defaulted.

feature10_3_encode_naming_strategy.json.description: When encoding, a naming strategy rewrites each property's program name to the chosen casing convention for the emitted keys.

feature11_encode_polymorphism.json.description: Encode a polymorphic value, writing its concrete type either as a YAML tag or as a discriminator property (default or custom name) according to the selected style.

feature12_parse_to_node.json.description: Parse a document into a generic node tree without a target type, exposing the structural shape (scalars, sequences, mappings, nulls and tagged nodes) and scalar contents.

cases.expected_output:
map (size: 2)
key 'text'
  scalar 'OK'
key 'node'
  tagged !!testtag
    scalar '2024-01-01'

feature13_null_document.json.description: Decoding the literal null document yields null for a nullable target and fails with a normalized unexpected-null error for a non-nullable target, across primitive, collection and object targets.

feature1_1_integer_bases.json.description: Decode a scalar document into an integer-like value, supporting decimal, hexadecimal (0x), octal (0o) and negative sign prefixes; the same textual forms decode identically regardless of the integer width requested.

feature1_2_floating_point.json.description: Decode a scalar document into a floating point value, including scientific notation and the special tokens for positive/negative infinity and not-a-number.

feature1_3_booleans.json.description: Decode a scalar document into a boolean value; only the lower/title/upper case spellings of true and false are accepted.

feature1_4_strings_and_chars.json.description: Decode a scalar document into a single string or single character value.

feature1_5_enums.json.description: Decode a scalar document into an enumeration value. Members may carry explicit external names (including names containing spaces). By default matching is case-sensitive; an option enables case-insensitive matching.

feature1_6_invalid_scalar.json.description: When a scalar cannot be interpreted as the requested type, decoding fails with a normalized invalid-scalar error carrying the offending raw value, a human description, and the 1-based line/column where it occurred.

cases.expected_output:
error=invalid_scalar
value=nonsense
detail=Value 'nonsense' is not a valid option, permitted choices are: Value1, Value2
line=1
column=1

error=invalid_scalar
value=abc
detail=Value 'abc' is not a valid integer value.
line=1
column=1

feature2_1_lists.json.description: Decode a block sequence document into a list whose elements are decoded with the requested element type (numbers, booleans, characters, nullable elements, nested lists, or objects).

feature2_2_maps.json.description: Decode a block mapping document into a key/value map with the requested key and value types.

feature3_1_simple_and_nested.json.description: Decode a mapping document into a structured object; nested objects and embedded lists are decoded recursively, and mapping keys may appear in any order.

input.document:
firstPerson:
  name: Alex
secondPerson:
  name: Jamie

feature3_2_optional_and_default.json.description: A field with a default value may be omitted from the document or supplied explicitly; when omitted the default is used.

input.document:
string: Alex
byte: 12
short: 1234
int: 123456
long: 1234567
float: 1.2
double: 2.4
enum: Value1
boolean: true
char: A
nullable: present

string: Alex
byte: 12
short: 1234
int: 123456
long: 1234567
float: 1.2
double: 2.4
enum: Value1
boolean: true
char: A

feature3_3_nullable_fields.json.description: A nullable field accepts an explicit null and yields a null value; a non-nullable field given null fails with a normalized invalid-property-value error reporting the null.

cases.expected_output:
error=invalid_property_value
property=name
reason=Unexpected null or empty value for non-null field.
line=1
column=7

feature3_4_missing_property.json.description: Decoding an object that omits a required (non-default) property fails with a normalized missing-property error naming the property and the location.

input.document:
byte: 12
short: 1234
int: 123456
long: 1234567
float: 1.2
double: 2.4
enum: Value1
boolean: true
char: A

feature3_5_unknown_property_strict.json.description: By default an unknown property fails with a normalized unknown-property error listing the known property names sorted alphabetically; disabling strict mode silently ignores unknown properties.

cases.expected_output:
error=unknown_property
property=extra-field
known=name
line=2
column=1
path=extra-field

feature3_6_invalid_property_value.json.description: When a property's value cannot be decoded as its declared type, decoding fails with a normalized invalid-property-value error naming the property, the reason, and the location.

input.document:
string: Alex
byte: xxx
short: 1234
int: 123456
long: 1234567
float: 1.2
double: 2.4
enum: Value1
boolean: true
char: A

cases.expected_output:
error=invalid_property_value
property=byte
reason=Value 'xxx' is not a valid byte value.
line=2
column=7

feature4_type_mismatch.json.description: Decoding a document whose shape does not match the requested type (a sequence where a scalar is expected, a mapping where a list is expected, etc.) fails with a normalized incorrect-type error describing what was expected versus what was found.

cases.expected_output:
error=incorrect_type
detail=Expected a string, but got a list
line=1
column=1

error=incorrect_type
detail=Expected a string, but got a map
line=1
column=1

feature5_naming_strategies_decode.json.description: An optional naming strategy maps a field's program name to the key spelled in the document (snake_case, kebab-case, PascalCase or camelCase); unknown keys are reported using the strategy-mapped known names.

feature6_anchors_aliases.json.description: Anchors (&name) and aliases (*name) are forbidden by default and rejected with a normalized error; when permitted they are resolved, and an optional maximum alias count guards against alias-expansion attacks by failing once the budget is exceeded. An extension-definition prefix marks anchor-defining keys at the document root.

cases.expected_output:
error=forbidden_anchor_or_alias
detail=Parsing anchors and aliases is disabled.
line=1
column=18

error=forbidden_anchor_or_alias
detail=Maximum number of aliases has been reached.
line=3
column=18

feature7_1_polymorphism_tag.json.description: With tag-based polymorphism, the concrete type of a value is selected from a YAML type tag (!<typeName>); the matching subtype is decoded, an untagged value fails with a normalized missing-type-tag signal, and an unrecognized tag fails with a normalized unknown-type error listing known types.

cases.expected_output:
error=invalid_property_value
property=element
reason=Value is missing a type tag (eg. !<type>)
line=2
column=3
cause=missing_type_tag

error=unknown_type
type=someOtherType
known=inlineString, sealedInt, sealedString
line=1
column=1

feature7_2_polymorphism_property.json.description: With property-based polymorphism, the concrete type is selected from a discriminator property (default name 'type', configurable); a missing discriminator fails with a normalized missing-property error and an unknown value fails with a normalized unknown-type error.

feature7_3_polymorphism_none.json.description: When polymorphism is disabled, a document that still carries a type tag or a discriminator-style mapping for a polymorphic target fails with a normalized incorrect-type error.

cases.expected_output:
error=incorrect_type
detail=Encountered a tagged polymorphic descriptor but PolymorphismStyle is 'None'
line=1
column=1

feature8_1_encode_primitives.json.description: Encode a primitive value to a YAML scalar document. Numbers and booleans are emitted plain; null is emitted as the plain token null.

feature8_2_encode_string_styles.json.description: Encode a string value choosing the quoting style: by default strings are double-quoted; single-quoted and plain styles are selectable; the plain-except-ambiguous style leaves unambiguous strings unquoted but quotes strings that could be misread as another type.

feature9_encode_collections.json.description: Encode a list either as a block sequence (default) or as an inline flow sequence; nested lists, maps and lists of objects follow the same chosen style.

_constraints.input_condition: All commands must be present in a single JSON object on stdin
_constraints.output_rule: The command must have exactly one of 'op', 'document', 'value' (and potentially 'shape', 'config')
The 'op' field must be one of 'decode', 'encode', or 'parse'
Returns either a compact JSON string or an error block
Returns the raw YAML text or an error block
Returns a human-readable indented node tree string or an error block
The 'shape' field selects the SchemaShapeCaptor metadata for type checking/encoding logic
Options govern encoding formatting and decoding behavior; missing config defaults to safe values stated in description
Default is 'block'. Can be set to 'flow' for inline [a, b] style
Default is 'double'. Options allow 'single', 'plain', or 'plain-except-ambiguous'

_constraints.input_condition: singleLineStringStyle is 'plain' or 'plain-except-ambiguous' AND the string is a numeric literal
_constraints.output_rule: Must be quoted because it is ambiguous (looks like a number), even with 'plain-except-ambiguous'

_constraints.input_condition: singleLineStringStyle is 'plain' AND the string is not ambiguous
_constraints.output_rule: Must remain unquoted

_constraints.input_condition: encoding an object and namingStrategy is provided
_constraints.output_rule: Program property names are remapped to yaml key names: snake, kebab, pascal, camel

_constraints.input_condition: A property in the value object equals its definition's default
_constraints.output_rule: Default: emit. If encodeDefaults=false, emit nothing (omit the key from output)

_constraints.input_condition: The input provided to decode is valid YAML structured text
_constraints.output_rule: Decode successful
Encode successful without a terminating newline (JSON structure defines block vs flow location)
First line of output must be: 'error=<category>'
Must include 'line' and 'column'. line must be 1-based. column must be 1-based.
Follow 'error=' line with newline-separated 'field=value' pairs describing the detail
Unknown or optional properties are silently ignored; no error emitted

_constraints.input_condition: shape is 'int' AND document contains 0x or 0o
_constraints.output_rule: The textual form is converted to the integer value regardless of the width ('int', 'long', etc) selected

_constraints.input_condition: shape is 'double' AND document contains .inf or .nan
_constraints.output_rule: .inf maps to
